channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese AI Police Tech Measures Vital Signs and "Core Motivation" at Beijing Expo
item.description: An international police equipment expo in Beijing showcased surveillance cameras that measure vital signs and even "core motivation" of suspects, reflecting China's advancing AI-powered security capabilities.

item.title: 🇺🇸 House Lawmakers Back 5-7% Military Pay Raise for 2027, No Increase for DoD Civilians
item.description: The House Armed Services Committee approved a 5% to 7% pay raise for service members in 2027, while Defense Department civilian employees would see no pay increase under the spending bill.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India's Urban Boom Turns Cities Into Heat Traps as Climate Change Intensifies
item.description: India's rapid urbanization is making cities significantly hotter, with the urban heat island effect combining with climate change to create dangerous living conditions for millions.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Announces Strait of Hormuz Closure After US Launches New Strikes
item.description: Iran's military declared the strategic Strait of Hormuz closed to all vessels and threatened to fire on any ships attempting transit, following fresh US military strikes on multiple Iranian targets.

item.title: 🇧🇭 Bahrain sounds missile alert sirens as Iran vows retaliation for US strikes
item.description: Bahrain activated its missile alert system as Iran announced it would retaliate after the US launched new airstrikes. The tiny Gulf kingdom hosts the US Fifth Fleet and is within range of Iranian missiles.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu to seek re-election despite Trump doubts
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu will run for re-election this year, his party announced, after US President Donald Trump said he was not sure if the Israeli prime minister would stand again.

item.title: 💰 Iran war anxiety doubles container shipping rates from Asia to US
item.description: The cost of shipping a container from Asia to the United States has doubled since the Iran war began, driven by soaring fuel prices and importers rushing to secure goods before costs rise further.

item.title: 🎯 Analysis suggests precision US strikes hit Iranian water facility
item.description: Satellite imagery and video analysis indicate US strikes may have hit a water treatment facility in Iran. It remains unclear whether the facility was deliberately targeted or struck inadvertently.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US launches fresh strikes on multiple targets in Iran as conflict escalates
item.description: The US military began a new round of strikes against multiple targets in Iran, hours after President Trump vowed further attacks if no peace deal is reached. The escalation comes amid ongoing tensions over Iran's nuclear program and attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine strikes Russian-occupied Mariupol port, disrupting Crimean supply lines
item.description: Ukrainian forces hit the port of Mariupol in a drone attack, Kyiv said, the latest in a series of strikes targeting logistics connecting Russia to Crimea. The attack follows reports of fuel shortages in Crimea due to Ukrainian drone operations.

item.title: 🇰🇷 US human rights officials meet North Korea detainee families in Seoul
item.description: Senior US human rights officials visiting South Korea met with families of South Koreans detained or abducted by North Korea, amid ongoing concerns about Pyongyang's human rights record and nuclear program.

item.title: 🇱🇧 UN to send investigators to Lebanon to assess potential war law violations
item.description: The UN human rights office will deploy a team of investigators to Lebanon next week to assess potential violations of international law by all parties during the ongoing conflict. The move comes amid continued Israeli strikes on Hezbollah targets.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese propagandists stoking dissent over Trump tariffs and data centres, OpenAI says
item.description: OpenAI reports that Chinese-speaking users were asked to use ChatGPT to create slogans and cartoons criticizing the US president's trade policies, highlighting ongoing influence operations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Pentagon's expanded blacklist of Chinese firms tests Xi's US visit
item.description: Washington's latest move highlights intense competition between the US and China, testing the fragile "stability" reached at the leadership summit in Beijing.

item.title: 💥🇮🇷 Massive new US strikes rock multiple targets inside Iran
item.description: Iranian state media networks confirm huge explosions across Bandar Abbas, Sirik and the nearby city of Minab in the southern part of the country amid a new wave of US attacks.

item.title: 💥🇮🇷 Explosions and airstrikes reported across southern Iran amid new US attacks
item.description: Explosions and airstrikes were reported across southern Iran early Thursday amid a new wave of US attacks, according to local reports.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine police chief: Russia recruits young women to kill Ukrainian servicemen
item.description: Ukraines police chief accused Russia of recruiting teenage Ukrainian girls to assassinate military personnel, following the arrest of a 17-year-old suspected of murdering a serviceman on instructions from a Russian operative.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China-North Korea claim wins from Xi visit, but limits remain
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinpings visit to North Korea elevated Kim Jong Uns global stature and pulled Pyongyang closer into Beijings orbit, though analysts note significant limits remain in the bilateral relationship.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump sticks with Pulte for intel job as risk grows of lapse in spy powers
item.description: A lapse in US foreign intelligence-gathering powers has grown more likely as President Trump continues to back his nominee for the intelligence post amid congressional gridlock.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in Southern Lebanon as Hezbollah Conflict Escalates
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people on Wednesday, Lebanese security sources said, as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah, with the Iran-backed group claiming fresh attacks against Israeli forces in the south.

item.title: 💰 US Inflation Hits Three-Year High at 4.2% as Iran War Drives Energy Prices Higher
item.description: Consumer prices rose at the fastest pace in three years in May, reaching 4.2% annually, as the Middle East conflict sent energy costs surging and gave the Federal Reserve ammunition to keep interest rates unchanged into 2027.

item.title: ☢️ UN Nuclear Watchdog Demands Iran Report Enriched Uranium Stocks
item.description: The IAEA's 35-nation Board of Governors passed a US-backed resolution telling Iran to declare its remaining enriched uranium stocks and allow inspectors to verify them, a move that could complicate Washington's ongoing talks with Tehran.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Erdogan warns Israel's attacks on Syria, Lebanon now threaten Turkey
item.description: Turkish President Erdogan stated that Israel's military operations in Syria and Lebanon have escalated to the point where they threaten Turkey itself, calling Israel's aggression a threat to the whole world.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine partners with Taurus missile maker for deep strike systems
item.description: Ukrainian defense company Ukrainian Armor and European firm MBDA initiated a strategic partnership to develop deep strike capabilities, enhancing Ukraine's indigenous defense production.

item.title: 🛢️ Countries vie to become energy safe haven as Hormuz crisis deepens
item.description: Multiple nations are pitching the Trump administration on energy alternatives as the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted, seeking to position themselves as secure energy suppliers.
